raw.population: [Rambeck et al.] N:178 (Germany) Age: 29.5±15.2 (6-72) WT:54.5±16.7 [Chiba et al.] N: 104 (Japan) Age: 6.05±3.94 WT: 22.9±11.6
raw.objective: “This paper reports our experience using NONMEM on some data, kindly supplied by other workers, concerning the clinical pharmacokinetics of phenytoin. The data were analyzed first by those who gathered them, and later by us. Comparing our results with theirs provides some insight into the usefulness of NONMEM and the ELS approach”
raw.observations: This study was performed to compare NONMEM output to results using other softwares. In general the parameter values obtained by NONMEM are similar to those found during earlier modelling.
raw.equations: [Rambeck et al.] Vo=theta1 VINT=theta2 SIZE=((WT/70)** theta4)*((HT/175) **theta5) Vm=(Vo*SIZE+VINT)*(1+eta1) Km=theta3*(1+eta2) DD=((Vm*Css)/(F*(Km+Css)))+eps1 [Chiba et al.] Vo=theta1 if(AGE<4)Vo=theta1*theta2 if(AGE>=4 & AGE<7)Vo= theta1*theta3 if(AGE>=7 & AGE<9)Vo= theta1*theta4 SIZE=exp(theta5*log(WT/70)+theta6*log(HT/175)) Vm=(Vo*SIZE)*(1+eta1) Ko=theta7 if(AGE<4)Ko= theta7*theta8 if(AGE>=4 & AGE<7)Ko= theta7*theta9 if(AGE>=7 & AGE<9)Ko= theta7*theta10 Km=Ko*(1+eta2) DD=((Vm*Css)/(F*(Km+Css)))+eps1

study.population: [Rambeck et al.] N:178 (Germany) Age: 29.5±15.2 (6-72) WT:54.5±16.7 [Chiba et al.] N: 104 (Japan) Age: 6.05±3.94 WT: 22.9±11.6
study.objective: “This paper reports our experience using NONMEM on some data, kindly supplied by other workers, concerning the clinical pharmacokinetics of phenytoin. The data were analyzed first by those who gathered them, and later by us. Comparing our results with theirs provides some insight into the usefulness of NONMEM and the ELS approach”

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
